text: Starlink provides backup connectivity to cell towers, ensuring that your phone works when you most need it https://t.co/SKaisMHka8
summary: Starlink provides backup connectivity to cell towers, ensuring that your phone works when you most need it
ai_summary: Elon Musk announces that Starlink is expanding its service to provide backup cellular connectivity to cell towers, enabling phones to maintain connectivity during outages. This represents a significant business pivot for Starlink—leveraging its satellite infrastructure into the terrestrial telecom market as a resilience solution—and demonstrates how SpaceX is monetizing satellite capacity beyond direct consumer internet service into critical infrastructure applications.
embed_html:
Starlink provides backup connectivity to cell towers, ensuring that your phone works when you most need it https://t.co/SKaisMHka8

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 18, 2026

ai_summary: Elon Musk announced that Grok, xAI's conversational AI model, is now available on Databricks, a major data and AI platform. This represents a significant distribution partnership that makes Grok accessible to Databricks' enterprise customer base, demonstrating xAI's strategy to integrate its AI capabilities into established enterprise infrastructure and expand Grok's market reach beyond direct channels.
embed_html:
Grok now on Databricks https://t.co/nTlVmQVcNd

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 18, 2026

ai_summary: A user requests voice command functionality for Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) system to enable natural conversation with Grok (xAI's AI assistant) for navigation decisions like 'turn right here' or 'drop us off at entrance.' Elon commits to delivering this conversational AI integration within approximately 3 months, indicating Tesla's near-term roadmap for combining autonomous driving with advanced natural language interfaces to improve user experience and vehicle control. This reflects the convergence of Tesla's FSD technology with xAI's Grok model to create more intuitive human-vehicle interaction.
embed_html:
This functionality will be there in about 3 months or so

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 18, 2026

ai_summary: SpaceX received a Baa1 investment-grade credit rating from Moody's (higher than Tesla's Baa3), with Moody's citing SpaceX's exceptional franchise strength as the world's leading orbital launch provider. Elon challenges the rating agencies' assessment of Tesla, noting the company holds over $40B in cash, carries no debt, and is consistently profitable—illustrating a disconnect between traditional credit rating methodologies and the actual financial strength of high-growth, capital-efficient companies. This discussion highlights how established rating frameworks may undervalue companies with unconventional balance sheets or business models, relevant to entrepreneurs seeking capital or understanding market perception of financial health.
embed_html:
Yeah, makes no sense.

Tesla has over $40B in cash, no debt and is consistently profitable!

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 19, 2026

ai_summary: SpaceX received a Baa1 investment-grade credit rating from Moody's, outperforming Tesla's Baa3 rating, with Moody's citing SpaceX's exceptional franchise strength as the world's leading orbital launch provider. Elon's commentary flags that Tesla's credit rating is disproportionately low relative to its business performance, highlighting a potential disconnect between market perception and operational fundamentals that operators should monitor regarding how capital markets value different business models.
